response.justification: Strong technical foundation with 20 years of software development and relevant developer tools experience (CI/CD setup, testing frameworks, MCP generators), but critically lacks the required 2+ years of people management experience. This is a full-time people-leadership role that demands proven ability to conduct 1-on-1s, manage performance, hire, and develop teams—none of which is evidenced in the resume. While the technical background is solid, the absence of demonstrated people management is disqualifying.
strengths: Deep technical expertise across full stack, embedded, and infrastructure domains with 20 years of diverse software development across multiple languages and platforms
Hands-on developer tools and infrastructure experience including CI/CD setup at Altom, testing framework design (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), and MCP code generators at re:cinq
Demonstrated ability to mentor and communicate technical knowledge through workshops, open-source contributions, and organizing technical learning sessions (refactoring dojos, Selenium courses)
concerns: No explicit people management experience—resume contains no evidence of managing teams, conducting performance reviews, hiring, developing direct reports, or leading organizational initiatives
No demonstrated strategic leadership or organizational change management at the scale required for a mid-to-senior management role
Limited focus on mobile platform ecosystem and build systems specific to large mobile platforms; experience scattered across many technologies rather than concentrated depth

key_factors.factor: Strong technical background and developer tools experience
No evidence of strategic organizational leadership
